title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Matthew Frieling
meeting_title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Leonid Shvorob and Matthew Frieling

transcript.text: How are you?
Yeah, I'm great.
Great.
Where are you based in?
Oh, last three years, I'm based in Bali, Indonesia, so now it's already like 3 a.m.
I know, I was going to say, it's so late there.
Do you work through the night typically?
Yeah, usually I'm working with U.S.
recruitment staffing companies, so usually I go to bed like 4 a.m.
Yeah.
Okay, nice.
I'm to put in my headphones here so I don't want disrupt it.
So, and you said you work with U.S.
companies primarily?
Yeah, recruitment staffing companies, so basically this.
So, I mentioned that you have very, like, very, very niche target, like Salesforce, SAP projects, right?
Yeah, exactly.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So what kind of these projects for you is most profitable but most difficult to source consistently?
Yeah, so we have an end client focus.
primarily we like to work with clients that are implementing SAP and or Salesforce.
We have, you know, we have worked with outsourced partners to support lead generation and intake of, you know, leads supporting our key objectives, which is end client focused.
So, you know, as an example, we had worked with a key partner that I had built a relationship with that had a good network in the SAP space.
you he was making introductions to.
To SAP America, we could get deeper connections and ties with SAP account executives as they were selling the S4HANA licenses to market.
And then we would be kind of looped in as a support mechanism to make sure those implementations go well.

And as an independent recruitment-focused, you know, SAP-focused recruitment firm, we kind of stand out in the market when it comes to that because a lot of organizations are maybe general IT or, you know, they don't really understand the SAP market the way that we do.
And that comes down to understanding specific niche skill sets, to delivering high-caliber talent that are truly independent, that don't work with partners, why that's important to our clients.

Client base is because a big part of what we offer is a truly independent model that actually puts key, you know, individuals and contributors in the client corner to offset risk with these implementations.

You know, a lot of companies, they, you know, they buy the licenses for SAP and then, you know, SAP introduces them to an implementation partner and they're off to the races and they're not thinking about how they maintain control of their SAP environment.
They're not thinking, they're just thinking, oh, okay, we have the partner that's going to execute.
And, you know, the partner has their agenda and their agenda is to put out as many people on that project as possible, to bill as much as possible, to enact change orders.
And if they don't have key resources.

In the client corner to maintain and stabilize their key objectives of the implementation, that completely gets drowned out by the agenda of the systems integrator, which is basically bill as much as possible, sell as many software licenses as possible.
So there's a lot of conflict of interest with these systems integrators.
Their intentions are to get the client successfully live on the platform, but the undercurrent of conflict is actually ends up being, you know, the primary objective of the systems integrator.
And what we do is we offset that risk in these implementations, which is why we have primarily an end client focus.
Do we deliver talent to partners?
Yes.
But we do, we're very picky.
We're not working with the IBMs.
They're the Accentures of the Deloitte's of the world, primarily because we have a brand protect, and our brand is, you know, end client focused.
Okay, end client, can you specify in more simple words?

Sure, okay, so an end client is like, let's say it's Hershey's is implementing SAP as an example, or you have like, maybe it's, I don't know, I can name any company out there that's implementing the application themselves, right?
So, you know, it could be, I don't know, name any company that's running, running SAP.
So, as I understand correctly, you have like, SAP and Salesforce engineers, and when company finished implementation.
And nobody can maintain it.
So you put your staff in the companies and then helping them to maintain it for several months.
And when everything's stabilized, they go to another project.
Right.
Or we bring in key people in the implementation.
So we have contractors that we put out to the market and they might be SAP talent, both on the business and engineering side.
It could be technical, could be functional.
And Salesforce, I primarily focus on the SAP space, but I do a lot of work in Salesforce too, because most of our clients that are running SAP also leverage Salesforce as their CRM.
So we, you know, we started Salesforce practice on the, on the back of our SAP bill, primarily because we had demand there in our, with our clients and we understood Salesforce.
We utilized it ourselves.
We'd become a partner to them.
We started implementing a Salesforce solutions as well.
But yeah, so two tracks, but primarily my targets are SAP.
Ideally, they run both SAP and Salesforce because that gives us a lot more leverage with the client for them to work with us.
But yeah, the end client focus is think it's not systems integrators, not IBM, Deloitte, Accenture.
Those are big partners.
They're going to have a lot of requirements out there.
And, you know, we could tie into them and support their growth, but our objective is to stay out of that track and stay focused on the client.
And the reason for that is because it eliminates conflict of interest.
We drive key...

Individuals into the client environment, whether that be program managers, key architects, and areas that are very important to the client, and support on the client side against DSI, against the Systems Integrator.
Okay, got you.
Yeah, it's very niche, so I think it's not many companies like yours on the market.
No.
think maybe only you or maybe a couple of others, competitors.
Yeah, we've been successful with it because, you know, maybe a decade ago, when we started the company back in 2013, no one really was doing this.
But, you know, my business partner, he had a vision for the company.
And it's, and now this model, after a decade, is becoming the norm.
of Like, organization.
Organizations see great value in talking to a partner that independently places talent in their teams on a contract, contract to hire, or direct hire model.

And the reason why that is is because organizations have had a lot of issues solely relying on the implementation partner because what they end up doing is that, you know, if you're interested, the reason why is because there's tons of lawsuits out there against Accenture's and IBM's and Deloitte's of the world because these implementations go sideways.
And it's, you know, we're not talking about, you it's like hundreds of millions of dollars that are lost in these implementations and nothing gets done or it's a failed implementation.
I mean, that's a lot of risk.
That's a lot of.

So now I'm like, okay, I'm exploring the idea of other options, but I mean, honestly, right now we're probably completely in a holding pattern as we develop the leads that we do have in front of us.
Just as we, you know, grow that market, 2025 was a hard year for us.
I mean, the U.S.
market, the tariffs, our clients had reorgs and layoffs and budget cuts.

So we're really in kind of a rebuild mode right now.

think this year is to be better for the U.S.
and all this stuff.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To explore a lead generation partnership for LocalWorld's niche staffing services.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=620.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**LocalWorld's Niche:** Staffing SAP/Salesforce talent for end-clients, not SIs (IBM, Accenture), to provide independent oversight and mitigate implementation risk.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=69.0)
  - [**Strategic Pause:** A previous lead-gen partner was paused due to poor ROI, making LocalWorld's CEO highly skeptical of new outsourced investments.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)
  - [**SystemHustle's Offer:** A 90-day pilot for $4k/mo, targeting 5–7 decision-maker intros by monitoring market signals (e.g., new leadership, project announcements) to ensure urgency.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=956.0)
  - [**Path Forward:** A partnership is not imminent. LocalWorld must first realize ROI from its previous investment. SystemHustle will provide case studies for future consideration.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)

## Topics

### LocalWorld's Niche & Strategy

  - [**Focus:** Staffing SAP & Salesforce talent for end-clients (e.g., Hershey's), not Systems Integrators (SIs) like IBM, Accenture, or Deloitte.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=69.0)
  - [**Value Proposition:** Provides independent oversight to mitigate SI-driven risks (e.g., scope creep, change orders) and protect client interests.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [**Target Market:** Mid-market companies ($500M–$7B annual revenue).](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Avoids the red tape and complexity of larger enterprises.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=852.0)
  - [**Project Scope:** Long-term engagements (6 months to multi-year), making client retention highly valuable.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=903.0)

### Strategic Pause & CEO Skepticism

  - [**Context:** A previous lead-gen partnership was paused in January due to poor ROI.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)
      - [**Root Cause:** Good leads were generated, but 2025 market conditions (reorgs, budget cuts) prevented closing.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=694.0)
  - [**Impact:** CEO Brendan is now highly skeptical of new outsourced investments.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1528.0)
  - [**Current Focus:** Internal BDRs are now working the existing lead pipeline to realize ROI before considering new partnerships.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)

### SystemHustle's Proposed Solution

  - [**Methodology:** Quality over quantity.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1002.0)
      - [Monitors market signals (new leadership, project announcements) to identify urgent needs.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)
      - [Targets decision-makers (e.g., IT Directors) with a custom, human-touch approach.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
  - [**Pilot Program:**](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1251.0)
      - [**Term:** 90 days.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1251.0)
      - [**Cost:** $4,000/month ($12,000 total).](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1312.0)
      - [**Goal:** 5–7 qualified decision-maker intros.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1251.0)
  - [**Long-Term Goal:** Build a retainer-based partnership focused on LTV and shared success.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1337.0)

### Path Forward

  - [**Timing:** A partnership is not imminent. Matthew must first demonstrate ROI from the previous investment to gain CEO Brendan's approval.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)
  - [**Requirement:** SystemHustle must prove its capability in the niche SAP/Salesforce market.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
  - [**Action:** SystemHustle will send case studies for Matthew to review and present when the timing is right.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1758.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Leonid (SystemHustle):**](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1758.0)
      - [Send relevant case studies to Matthew.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1758.0)
      - [Connect with Matthew on WhatsApp to maintain contact.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1862.0)
  - [**Matthew (LocalWorld):**](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)
      - [Review materials and present the partnership concept to CEO Brendan when the timing is appropriate.](https://fathom.video/share/zWnZz-gGGzoQZwsi-w_bx24TiUxLR2QG?tab=summary&timestamp=1804.0)
